rules.label: World Readable File Permissions
rules.description: `os.OpenFile(path, flag, 0666)` or `os.WriteFile(path, data, 0777)` with world-readable/writable permissions
rules.explanation: In Go, World Readable File Permissions matters because the pattern here, `os.OpenFile(path, flag, 0666)` or `os.WriteFile(path, data, 0777)` with world-readable/writable permissions, is worth review because transport and persistence code often hide the exact boundary where data becomes dangerous or sensitive.
rules.fix: For World Readable File Permissions in Go, use explicit safe APIs, validate untrusted input close to the edge, and keep security-sensitive behavior in reviewed shared helpers.

rules.label: Xml Decoder without Entity Limit
rules.description: `xml.NewDecoder(r)` processing untrusted XML without setting `d.Entity = nil` and without input size limits
rules.explanation: In Go, Xml Decoder without Entity Limit matters because the pattern here, `xml.NewDecoder(r)` processing untrusted XML without setting `d.Entity = nil` and without input size limits, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Xml Decoder without Entity Limit in Go, add setting `d.Entity = nil` and without input size limits or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.

rules.label: Yaml Unmarshal Untrusted Input
rules.description: `yaml.Unmarshal(untrustedInput, &target)` using `gopkg.in/yaml.v2` without size limits
rules.explanation: In Go, Yaml Unmarshal Untrusted Input matters because the pattern here, `yaml.Unmarshal(untrustedInput, &target)` using `gopkg.in/yaml.v2` without size limits, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Yaml Unmarshal Untrusted Input in Go, add size limits or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.
